purpose: Implements CopyHtmlToClipboard. Builds a CF_HTML payload (Version:0.9 header with StartHTML/EndHTML/StartFragment/EndFragment offsets pointing at a <!--StartFragment-->/<!--EndFragment-->-wrapped <article class="markdown-body">), registers the 'HTML Format' clipboard format, and writes both that HTML payload and a CF_UNICODETEXT fallback to the clipboard via GlobalAlloc/SetClipboardData.
exports: markdownplusplus::CopyHtmlToClipboard(HWND, const std::wstring& articleHtml, const std::wstring& plainText) -> bool
invariants: The CF_HTML header offsets must be computed AFTER the header is sized; they are byte offsets from the start of the entire CF_HTML buffer, not from the inner HTML payload. The %09zu zero-padded fields keep the header length constant so the two-pass size computation works.
GlobalAlloc handles passed to SetClipboardData are transferred to the system on success - do NOT GlobalFree them in that case; only free on the failure path (this file does that correctly).
OpenClipboard/CloseClipboard must always be paired; CopyHtmlToClipboard does so.
gotchas: If both SetClipboardHtml and SetClipboardUtf16Text fail the function returns false but the clipboard has already been emptied - callers should treat false as a hard failure.
The CF_HTML payload is UTF-8 even though Windows clipboard text APIs prefer UTF-16; that is per the HTML Format spec.
